question: Does the generated text include three distinct sections covering 'Design Philosophy and Materials', 'Outdoor Application Scenarios', and 'Ultimate Portability'?
Is the generated text written in the format of a verbatim product pitch script suitable for an investor presentation (i.e., professional, persuasive, spoken-word style)?
Does the text in the 'Design Philosophy' section explicitly mention the 'canvas' and 'aluminum' materials as labeled in the input sketch?
Does the output contain exactly three images, with one image placed within or immediately adjacent to each of the three text sections?
Does the first generated image depict the chair in a minimalist pure white studio setting?
Does the second generated image show the chair on a sunny outdoor camping lawn with a tent visible nearby?
Does the third generated image depict the chair in a fully folded state being carried by a model on a city street?
Do the generated images successfully transform the hand-drawn sketch style into realistic, commercial-quality photography?
Do all generated images consistently depict the specific chair design shown in the input sketch (e.g., tubular metal frame, X-frame structure, canvas seat)?
Does the folded chair in the third image accurately reflect the 'Compact Pack' configuration illustrated in the input sketch?
Are the images interleaved logically, appearing immediately adjacent to the specific text section they illustrate (e.g., Studio shot with Design section, Street shot with Portability section)?
Do the generated images provide informative visual evidence that supports the text claims (e.g., showing the texture of materials or the ease of carrying)?
